item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this. When I started reading it got me interested into this straight away. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi there, leaving a plus one as i think what is there is decent on it's own and i enjoyed reading it but i do think it can be improved on though without changing the structure too much as you suggested. I'm not SCP expert so i'll just look at it from a narrative point of view, i think it either needs some further…development of the mystery of this thing as to interviews with locals maybe, or perhaps another exploration log investigating further into what it..wants, or at least some kind of extra depth.

Again, i'm no writing expert but that's just my feeling on finishing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey Akimoto, thanks for the crit.

I understand this is kind of "floaty" in terms of language used and it needs some more clinical-ness. When writing this I wanted to avoid strict and precise language in order to not make the entity feel predictable, but in hindsight I've probably gone a little far with that.

Next, the generic nature of this. I feel I could definitely have improved on that and shaped this into something more unique, but I really just wanted to write something basic. Something simple but scary. To me, the main problem with this skip is it's a little too generic, a little too vague, and overall boring, as you said.

I've made some crude edits here and there to address some of the issues you mentioned, but I don't think I can make this as good as possible without completely changing the structure of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, Azamo, let's take a look at whatchu got:

examples
examples

SCP-5152 itself is currently uncontainable; however, this is not a serious issue as visitors to the region it inhabits are rare.

"however, this is not a serious issue.." this sentence isn't very clinical.

An exclusion zone has been established roughly 4km in excess of SCP-5152-1 for increased security, and is to be increased in size accordingly if SCP-5152 leaves it.

Again clinical tone is lacking, instead of roughly try approximately, "in excess" is an odd way of saying it, and "leaves it" could be phrased better. And you don't need a comma after security.

The exact perimeter of SCP-5152-1 has not been determined due to the nature of SCP-5152's movement, and it is not known if SCP-5152 is capable of leaving it, but as of the present, recorded sightings elsewhere have not occurred.

Run-on sentence.

SCP-5152 is generally only visible to a maximum of one individual at any time, although it occasionally makes itself known to small groups.

How does it do this? Is it literally invisible or does it just stay hidden?

If SCP-5152 is near a group of individuals it will typically remain a large distance away and silently "observe" them for a long period,

Large distance is an odd way of putting it. Also, why is observe I quotes?


I just pointed out a few things but the conprocs and description could both use a good going over for the poor clinical tone and weird phrasing.

The dialogue itself is stiff and unnatural in spots and borderline goofy in others. This inconsistency makes it hard to take it seriously in either direction, so it just kind falls flat altogether.

So, weird phrasing and tones aside, this is kind of just boring? You tell us what it is and what it does then show it doing that– in a not so interesting way. With an anomaly as generic as this, the story and the execution of that story are most important, in my opinion, this fall short of all of it.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to DrAkimoto or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
damn, 0 rating lol
let's hope this doesn't die! if it goes below -2 i'll take it off and try to fix it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some slight tonal issues, but that aside it's real good. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
for some reason this SCP remind me of SCP 2521 although dont know why, but still is really cool

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Hey, everyone!

I'm aware this one might feel a little cliche, but I wanted to write something based on a couple of my fears: being followed while alone and weird dark snowy forests. Quite the mixture, but I think it definitely works to some degree, even if it may feel slightly Series 1 for some readers.

I want to thank The Great Hippo and a couple others on the various SCP discords for critting this skip and helping me make it more spooky.
